title: BWC Request: Jay Cutler — Former NFL Quarterback Arrested for DUI After Rear-Ending a Car in

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Public Records Request — Body-Worn Camera Footage: Jay Cutler

To Whom It May Concern,

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act (T.C.A. § 10-7-503), I am requesting access to the following public records:

All body-worn camera (BWC) footage, dashcam footage, and any other audio or video recordings related to the following incident:

Case/Incident: Jay Cutler — Former NFL Quarterback Arrested for DUI After Rear-Ending a Car in Franklin TN
Date of Incident: September 30, 2024
Agency: Franklin Police Department
Summary: Franklin TN, October 2024. Former Chicago Bears QB Jay Cutler rear-ends a car while drunk. Officers arrive at scene, immediately notice alcohol smell. Cutler first denies drinking, then says "a little...


Note: I understand this footage has already been released to media outlets. I am requesting the same footage be provided directly to me as a member of the public under the applicable records law.

This request includes:
1. All body-worn camera footage from any officers involved in the incident
2. All dashcam / in-car camera footage from responding vehicles
3. Any booking room, interview room, or holding area footage related to this incident
4. Any associated arrest reports or incident reports (if not exempt)

I request that this footage be provided electronically if possible (via download link, USB, or email attachment).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption being claimed.

Thank you for your assistance.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sam Nkata

title: BWC Request: Sophia Ross — Hooters Waitress Calls Cop "Daddy," Offers "Anything" to Avoid DUI

Summary: Michael J. Esquilin steals an ambulance in Florida March 16-17 2025 and is caught on camera chugging beer inside the stolen vehicle during the pursuit. Florida Highway Patrol bodycam and dashcam foota...

title: Request for Body Cam Footage of DUI Arrest on March 20, 2024

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Incident Details for Footage Retrieval:

Date: March 20, 2024
Location: Montgomery County, Maryland
Suspect: Karen Huger, 61
Incident Summary: Huger was arrested for DUI after crashing her Maserati into a median and two signs. Reports indicate she was highly intoxicated, appeared incoherent, and made unusual statements to officers on scene. Bodycam footage from her arrest was later released, showing her referring to herself as "Thomas Jefferson's concubine" and claiming she was "extremely intelligent." She was charged with DUI, DWI, and reckless driving.
I am requesting copies of all body-worn camera footage from officers who:

Responded to the scene of Huger’s crash.
Conducted any field sobriety tests or questioning.
Placed her under arrest and transported her to detention.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sam Nkata
